title: Search for Missing Crew After Ship Overturns in Typhoon
summary: A cargo ship called the Mariana overturned near the Northern Mariana Islands during a powerful typhoon. The ship had six crew members on board. After the storm, searchers found the body of one crew member. They are still looking for the other five. The searchers used a special underwater drone and divers to check inside the ship. They also looked for a life raft that might have floated away. The typhoon, named Sinlaku, was very strong with winds up to 150 miles per hour. Many people from different countries helped in the search, covering an area as big as the state of Oregon. The ship had lost one of its engines during the storm and asked for help, but then contact was lost. Everyone hopes the missing crew will be found safe.

background_read: The Northern Mariana Islands are a group of islands in the Pacific Ocean. They are a territory of the United States. Typhoons are like hurricanes but happen in the Pacific. They can be very dangerous for ships at sea.
Article_Structure: WHO: A cargo ship called Mariana with six crew members, searchers from the U.S. Coast Guard, Japan, and other countries.
WHAT: The ship overturned during a typhoon. One crew member's body was found, and five others are missing.
WHERE: Near the Northern Mariana Islands in the Pacific Ocean.
WHY: The ship was hit by Super Typhoon Sinlaku, a very strong storm with high winds.

perspectives.description: They are leading the search and rescue mission.
They are worried and hope their loved ones are found safe.
